data.title: Alibaba, Baidu invest in Horizon Robotics' Hong Kong IPO
data.description:
Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) and Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) have emerged as cornerstone investors in Hong Kong's upcoming initial public offering of Horizon Robotics, a Chinese company specializing in autonomous driving technology, Bloomberg reported.

Intel-backed (INTL) Horizon Robotics is a provider of software and hardware used in autonomous driving systems and is aiming to raise HK$5.4 billion ($696 million) by selling 1.36 billion shares at HK$3.73 to HK$3.99 each.

French shipping billionaire Rodolphe Saade’s CMA CGM and a Ningbo government fund are also cornerstone investors. Together, these cornerstone investors have subscribed for a total of ~$220 million in stock led by Alisoft China (BABA) and Baidu (BIDU), which have bid for $50 million each.

Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) also has a small stake in the company. Other backers of the IPO include China’s BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) and Contemporary Amperex Technology.

Horizon Robotics was founded in 2015 and is managed by Kai Yu, a scientist with expertise in artificial intelligence who was a key player in Baidu's (BIDU) push for autonomous driving.

The company is seeking a valuation of up to $6 billion before including the funds raised in the IPO, people with knowledge of the matter said. This positions it as one of the largest listings in Hong Kong this year.

The company will begin accepting orders on Wednesday, October 16. The discussions are still in progress, and specifics like the IPO's size could change.

data.title: Nel ASA reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Nel ASA press release (OTCPK:NLLSF): Q3 GAAP EPS of -NOK0.07.
  • Revenue of NOK366M (+20.8% Y/Y).
  • EBITDA in the quarter was NOK -90 million (Q3 2023: -62). Alkaline had a positive contribution to Group EBITDA but this was more than offset by the negative contribution from PEM.
  • Net loss from continuing operation was NOK -115 million (Q3 2023: -167). The improvement was mainly explained by the NOK -90 million fair value adjustment from shareholdings in Everfuel in Q3 2023.
  • Order intake in the quarter amounted to NOK 161 million, a 52% decrease from the corresponding quarter last year (Q3 2023: 338).
  • Order backlog was NOK 1 872 million at the end of the quarter, down 20% from the third quarter of 2023 and down 10% from the previous quarter.
  • Cash balance was NOK 1 941 million at quarter end (Q3 2023: 3 799)

data.title: UK's inflation drops to 1.7% in September
data.description:
• Inflation rate in the UK decreased to 1.70% in September from 2.20% in August, lower than consensus estimates of 1.7%.

  • The Consumer Price Index in the UK decreased 0% in September 2024 over the previous month. 

  • ETFs: (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB).

    Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: LeddarTech gets $3M bridge financing
data.description:
• An automotive software company LeddarTech (NASDAQ:LDTC) received the second tranche of the bridge loans in an aggregate amount of $3M, which are part of a bridge financing in an aggregate amount of up to $9M made available to the company by certain of its principal shareholders, namely FS Investors, Investissement Québec and its senior lender, Fédération des caisses Desjardins du Québec.

  • The principal details of the Bridge Financing were announced by the company on August 19, 2024.

  • The bridge financing is comprised of two tranches, with the first tranche of $6M funded on August 19, 2024. The second tranche of the Bridge Financing, in an amount of up to $3M.

data.title: Embraer to invest up to $70M commercial jet facilities in Texas
data.description:
• Embraer (NYSE:ERJ) said that the expansion and investment in the U.S. of its maintenance, repair and overhaul services network to support the growing fleet of E-Jets with the opening of a new Embraer owned service center at the Perot Field Alliance Airport, in Fort Worth, Texas.
  • The investment will reach up to $70M and is expected to create approximately 250 new aviation jobs in Texas.
  • In partnership with the City of Fort Worth, Denton County and the State of Texas, Embraer expects to begin operations in an existing hangar, in the beginning of Q2 2025, while building a second hangar that should be concluded by 2027.

data.title: Guidewire Software prices $600M senior notes
data.description:
• Guidewire Software (NYSE:GWRE) priced its offering of $600M aggregate principal amount of 1.25% convertible senior notes due 2029 in a private offering.
  • The offering size was increased from the previously announced offering size of $50M aggregate principal amount of notes.
  • The issuance and sale of the notes are scheduled to settle on October 18, 2024.
  • Initial purchasers have an option to purchase, for settlement within a period of 13 days from, and including, the date the notes are first issued, up to an additional $90M notes.
  • Net proceeds to it from the offering will be ~$583.7M.

data.title: Nikkei leads declines in Asia-Pacific markets mixed trade, tracking tech-led selloff on Wall Street
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets trade mostly lower, with Japan’s Nikkei leading losses, following declines on Wall Street as chip stocks tumbled after Dutch giant ASML Holding cut its annual sales forecast.

The Japan (NKY:IND) fall -1.92% to below 39,300 on Wednesday, reversing gains from the previous session. The Japanese yen stabilized around 149 per dollar as investors reacted to the Bank of Japan board member Seiji Adachi’s latest comments.

Adachi stated that monetary policy normalization is underway, but emphasized the need for moderate interest rate increases and cautioned against drastic policy changes due to uncertainties in global economic outlook and domestic wage growth.

Domestically, investors digested data showing machinery orders in Japan fell more than expected in August, with both the manufacturing and non-manufacturing sectors seeing declines.

Japan's core machinery orders, which exclude those for ships and electric power companies, declined by 1.9% month-on-month to 858.1 billion yen in August 2024.

China (SHCOMP) rose +0.40% to around 3,185 while the Shenzhen Component dropped 1% to 9,960 on Wednesday, extending losses from the previous session as investors cautiously awaited stimulus announcements in China, with the housing minister set to hold a press briefing on Thursday.

The People's Bank of China injected CNY 642.4 billion through seven-day reverse repos on October 16, resulting in a net cash withdrawal of CNY 207.6 billion for the day due to expiring MLF loans.

Investors also look ahead to a raft of key economic indicators later this week including GDP, retail sales and industrial production figures.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose +0.88% to 20,407 in early trade on Wednesday, ending declines in the prior two sessions amid gains from property and financials.

Meanwhile, Hong Kong leader John Lee will deliver his annual policy address today. He is expected to lay out plans to boost the city's economy, including a potential cut to a liquor tax.

India (SENSEX) fall -0.02%

Australia (AS51) fall -0.28% to below 8,290 on Wednesday, retreating slightly from all-time highs.

The Westpac-Melbourne Institute Leading Economic Index in Australia was almost flat month-over-month in September, staying muted for the sixth month running.

The NZX 50 lost 61 points, or 0.5%, to 12,780 around midday on Wednesday, halting gains from the prior session after New Zealand's inflation rate eased to 2.2% in Q3 of 2024, bringing it within the RBNZ's 1-3% target range for the first time since Q1 of 2021.

In the U.S., on Tuesday, all three major indexes ended in red driven by weak earnings from ASML, which led to a broad selloff in chipmakers, and a sharp decline in oil prices that pressured energy stocks.

U.S. stock futures were little changed on Wednesday after the Dow and S&P 500 retreated from record highs in the previous session: Dow flat; S&P 500 +0.11%; Nasdaq +0.22%.

Investors are now looking forward to additional economic data, including retail sales reports and speeches from several Fed officials, for further guidance.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

  • China’s plan to stimulate economic growth offers scant details

  • China's Sep CPI hits three-month low at 0.4% and PPI drops to lowest level in six months

  • China announces special bond to support economy, no major stimulus

  • RBA minutes: Board discussed future cases for raising, lowering rates, stays watchful on inflation risks

  • Japan appears to be on the path of a sustainable rebound – Vanguard

data.title: Wednesday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
7:00 AM MBA Mortgage Applications

The data point measures the change in the number of new applications for mortgages backed by the MBA during the reported week. Mortgage applications fell for the second week, ending 5.1% lower for the week ending October 4 after the benchmark 30-year fixed mortgage rate rallied 22 bps to 6.36%.

8:30 AM Import/Export Prices

The Import/Export Price Indexes contain data on changes in the prices of nonmilitary goods and services traded between the U.S. and the rest of the world. The imports price is set to decline by 0.3% in September.

11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement

The U.S. Treasury will announce buyback at 11:00 AM. Buybacks are a cash management tool to give the government flexibility to manage the public debt.

2:00 PM Treasury Buyback Results

Institutions that are approved by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York (FRBNY) to conduct open market transactions may participate in a buyback. FRBNY accepts offers with the best value relative to prevailing market prices. Shortly after the operation, Treasury publishes summary results.

Click here to see the weekly economic calendar.

data.title: Rio Tinto reports slightly higher Q3 iron ore output, warns on inflation
data.description:
Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) said Tuesday it produced slightly more iron ore in Q3 while narrowly missing expectations, while copper and aluminum output came in lower compared to the year-earlier quarter.

The world's largest producer of iron ore reported Q3 production from its Pilbara operations in Western Australia of 84.1M metric tons in the three months to September 30, up 1% Y/Y and up 6% from Q2.

Q3 iron ore shipments, most of which is destined for steel mills in China, rose 1% Y/Y and 5% Q/Q to 84.5M tons, slightly below a Visible Alpha consensus estimate of 84.7M tons; Q2 results were hurt by low portside inventories and the effects of a train collision.

Rio (RIO) reaffirmed its FY 2024 Pilbara iron ore shipments forecast of 323M-338M tons, with mining costs anticipated in the upper half of its annual forecast of $21.75-$23.50/ton, as inflation runs at the higher end of the company's expectations.

Q3 aluminum output fell 2% Y/Y to 809K tons, as production in New Zealand was impacted by a call to reduce electricity usage at its smelter from early August which has since ended, and Rio (RIO) said the smelter will ramp back up through to Q2 2025.

Q3 mined copper production slipped 1% to 168K tons, with output from its Kennecott site in Utah sinking 44% Y/Y as highwall movement was monitored along two major faults during the period; the company reworked its mine plan to manage the risk, but it expects mined copper production will be affected in 2025 and 2026.

data.title: Williams wins again in Louisiana pipeline legal dispute with Energy Transfer
data.description:
The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission rejected Energy Transfer's (ET) request to review Williams Companies' (NYSE:WMB) Louisiana Energy Gateway pipeline, Bloomberg reported Monday, in the latest ruling in a long-running legal dispute between the two companies.

Energy Transfer (ET) had asked FERC to treat Louisiana Energy Gateway as an interstate pipeline and take over regulatory responsibility; Williams (WMB) argued the pipeline is an intrastate line outside FERC's jurisdiction, a position previously affirmed by a state court, which also ruled in Williams' favor in a right-of-way dispute involving Louisiana Energy Gateway's crossing of an existing Energy Transfer pipeline.

"We deny Energy Transfer's petition for the Commission's issuance of an order to Williams to show cause," FERC said in its September 27 ruling, which clears the path for Williams (WMB) to proceed with the construction and operation of the 1.8B cf/day pipeline as planned.

"With this ruling, Williams continues to prevail against Energy Transfer's efforts to hinder the development of our project," a Williams (WMB) spokesperson said.
